title: STUDY TOPIC: - Nativity part 6
devotional:
Matthew 1:18-25

‘’This is how the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about. His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit. Because Joseph her husband was faithful to the law, and yet did not want to expose her to public disgrace, he had in mind to divorce her quietly but after he had considered this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit. She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.” All this took place to fulfil what the Lord had said through the prophet: “The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel” (which means “God with us”). When Joseph woke up, he did what the angel of the Lord had commanded him and took Mary home as his wife. But he did not consummate their marriage until she gave birth to a son. And he gave him the name Jesus.’’

I wonder if you have ever been deeply challenged or disturbed by the ‘Christmas’ story. It’s a story we all know, and that is perhaps a problem, because we know it so well it has become ‘Disney-tised.’ We have sanitised it to make it more palatable.

But if we were to take a closer look we will discover that the Christmas story is about difficult relationships, frustrated plans, divine interruptions, unfavourable circumstances, hard choices, insecure authorities, cruel atrocities, unexpected visitors. And yet despite all this, the story of Christmas teaches us how to live with God’s perspective on life and it teaches a lot about building a family where God feels loved.

Joseph and Mary did not live in some kind of utopia where it was easy to love God. Life was difficult, demanding, uncomfortable and sometimes uncertain, but because of what was in their hearts, their home and ‘marriage’ became somewhere where God felt loved. We started to look at Joseph and how God dealt with him so he could be a father to Jesus. We want to consider him and see how his commitment to God worked itself out in his family. I think this is the most important thing about our Christian life, how is it worked out in the context of our family life.

1. Joseph needed to see Mary the way God saw her.

When the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, Joseph was able to have a different and new perspective about Mary. Joseph listened to what God had to say and this had a dramatic positive effect on his relationship with Mary. We read ‘Joseph son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.’

Joseph needed to get past all his cultural upbringing and his rigid understanding of the law, if he was going to see Mary as God saw her. If he was going to really love Mary, then he needed to continually see her from God perspective.

How we see people is the number one test of our spirituality. How we see others and how we relate to them, especially in the family context is the most accurate indicator of our spirituality.

Let’s think through the consequences of this. What difference would it make if we saw our spouse as God saw them? To see your spouse as God sees them is to recognise their value, it is the celebrate their worth, it is to be filled with gratitude for them, it is seeing that they are to be loved unconditionally.

We live in a culture where people think it is funny or clever to belittle or demean their spouses. This not only happens in non-Christians homes, but sadly in Christian homes also. In some ‘Christian’ homes there are wives who hide behind a false comfort, they say to themselves, something like, ’but he doesn’t hit me.’ and they choose to live in a dark secret world of emotional abuse where they feel robbed of dignity, respect and self worth, and because emotional abuse does not leave physical scars, it can almost become acceptable, it is tolerated. This also happens to some men, who are emotionally and verbally abused.

Emotional abuse is not someone losing their temper in an argument or having a heated disagreement with you on occasion. It is when your normal becomes a regular pattern of disrespectful, hurtful, demeaning, manipulative behaviour.

Emotional abuse often uses verbal abuse, which could include angry outbursts, screaming, swearing, ridicule, name calling, blaming, accusation, criticism, threats, orders, mockery, manipulation, coercion, put-downs, shaming, word twisting, rewriting history, and attacking personal character.

Jesus categorizes verbal abuse as a serious offence with eternal consequences: “You have heard that it was said to the people long ago, ‘You shall not murder, and anyone who murders will be subject to judgment.’ But I tell you that anyone who is angry with a brother or sister will be subject to judgment. Again, anyone who says to a brother or sister, ‘Raca,’ [an Aramaic term of contempt meaning “worthless, empty”] is answerable to the court. And anyone who says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of hell. Matthew 5:22.

God’s desire is that we “encourage one another and build each other up” (1 Thessalonians 5:11) and that we “do not let any unwholesome talk come out of [our] mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs” (Ephesians 4:29).

God intends that our words and our relationships be healthy and life giving. Encouragement must be intentional, it is something we should consciously choose to do. Some people say, ‘encouragement is not my gift,’ that is like saying ‘love is not my responsibility.’

In seeing Mary from God’s perspective meant that Joseph was able to marry her, and become a human father to Jesus.
prayer: Father help us to always view our spouse as you do.
html:
STUDY TOPIC: - Nativity part 6

Study Text: Ezra 1

Matthew 1:18-25

‘’This is how the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about. His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit. God intends that our words and our relationships be healthy and life giving. Encouragement must be intentional, it is something we should consciously choose to do. Some people say, ‘encouragement is not my gift,’ that is like saying ‘love is not my responsibility.’

In seeing Mary from God’s perspective meant that Joseph was able to marry her, and become a human father to Jesus.

Prayer: Father help us to always view our spouse as you do.
